title: Club Café founder and vice president dies, the venue says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Frank A. Ribaudo, founder of Club Café, died after battling brain cancer for two and a half years.
Ribaudo transformed Club Café into a hub for LGBTQ+ community and HIV/AIDS advocacy in Boston.
He also co-founded the Harbor to the Bay AIDS Benefit Bike Ride and promoted LGBTQ+ athletics and support funds.

title: Boston man charged with snatching older women’s purses while riding by them on moped
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Daneuris Diaz, 22, charged with 22 counts related to nine robberies targeting older women in Boston.
Diaz allegedly robbed victims while on a moped, injuring some who fell during the incidents.
Authorities traced stolen credit card use to Diaz and found matching clothing and gloves linking him to the crimes.

title: Norwell issues town-wide boil water order after E. coli detected in water supply
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Norwell issued a boil water order after E. coli was detected in the town's water supply on July 7.
Town officials are flushing water mains, increasing chlorination, and distributing bottled water to address contamination.
E. coli symptoms include diarrhea and nausea; high-risk groups include infants and immunocompromised individuals.

title: Willson Contreras to become 1st Red Sox participant in Home Run Derby since 2011
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Willson Contreras will be the first Red Sox player in 15 years to participate in the Home Run Derby.
The Home Run Derby takes place on July 13 at Philadelphia’s Citizens Bank Park with Contreras representing the Red Sox.
Contreras has hit 20 home runs in 88 games and is a replacement for Toronto's Vladimir Guerrero Jr. in the All-Star events.

title: Democrat Graham Platner says he plans to withdraw from Maine Senate race after sexual assault claim
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Graham Platner plans to withdraw from the Maine Senate race after a sexual assault claim, affecting Democratic unification efforts.
Platner's exit follows a report alleging he drunkenly forced a woman into a non-consensual encounter in 2021.
Democrats will hold a nominating convention to replace Platner, with a new candidate required by July 27.

title: Elias Lindholm remains an uncomfortable question mark for retooling Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Elias Lindholm's performance with the Boston Bruins has declined due to injuries and underwhelming production, causing uncertainty for the team.
Lindholm's $7.75 million cap hit and five-year contract pose challenges as the Bruins evaluate their center depth chart.
The Bruins might shift Lindholm to a third-line role due to younger players emerging as more appealing top-six options.

title: Boston Councilor Julia Mejia suffers medical emergency during City Council meeting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston City Councilor Julia Mejia experienced a medical emergency during a City Council meeting at City Hall.
The meeting was paused for about 30 minutes while Mejia was transported by EMS to a Boston-area hospital.
Mayor Michelle Wu reported that Councilor Mejia is doing well and wished her a swift recovery.

title: My truck’s air conditioner only works when set to full blast
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2007 Ford F-150's AC issue may be due to a faulty blower motor resistor, costing $20-$65 to replace.
A 2011 Infiniti G25x exhibits sluggish performance post-repair; possible undiagnosed issues could include damaged sensors or wiring.
A 2017 Jeep Compass Trailhawk experiences start/stop system issues, likely requiring diagnostics for battery or system faults.

title: ‘Nine amazing years’: Jayson Tatum on his relationship with Jaylen Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown played together on the Boston Celtics for nine years, winning a title in 2024.
Brown was recently traded to the 76ers; Tatum expressed surprise and deep respect for Brown during a recent interview.
The Celtics traded Brown for Paul George and multiple draft picks to manage their salary cap under new NBA rules.

title: 4 standout moments from Tom Brady’s appearance on New Heights
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tom Brady appeared on the 'New Heights' podcast discussing his career, including a memorable hit by Nate Clements.
Brady highlighted Randy Moss's notable catch in 2007 as a turning point for the Patriots that season.
Brady dismissed the Belichick vs. Brady debate, calling it irrelevant and emphasizing the team's collaborative success.
